ip_outcome_note: Depicted figure confirmed as Nymble (National Pokedex no. 919) via PokemonTCG.io API (nationalPokedexNumbers field) and card name. Dark blue cricket-like insect with orange circular eye marking matches canonical Nymble design. Grass-type card typing corroborates species (Nymble is a Bug-type in the video games; the TCG prints it as Grass-type per its type-mapping conventions).

trivia_bullets.text: Nymble (no. 009/094) appears in the Mega Evolution—Phantasmal Flames expansion (set code me2 / PFL), released November 14, 2025 — the second main expansion of the Mega Evolution Series. The set is headlined by Mega Charizard X ex and Mega Gengar ex. Phantasmal Flames contains 130 cards including 36 secret rares. [PokemonTCG.io API; pokemon.com Phantasmal Flames announcement]

Nymble is National Pokedex no. 919, a Bug-type Pokemon introduced in Generation IX (Scarlet/Violet). It evolves into Lokix (no. 920), which has seen rogue competitive play: a Lokix (PFL no. 10) list reached the top 16 of a City League tournament in October 2025, built around Lokix's 'Jumping Shot' attack that shuffles the attacker back into the deck after hitting. [PokemonTCG.io API; limitlesstcg.com/cards/PFL/10; pokemondb.net/pokedex/nymble]

'It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokemon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.' — flavor text, Nymble (Phantasmal Flames 009). This directly mirrors the card's lone attack, Flail Around (coin-flip damage) — the flavor establishes why a creature this small can generate unpredictable bursts: compacted kinetic energy in the folded legs. [PokemonTCG.io API]

Illustrated by Yuka Morii, one of the TCG's most recognized long-tenure artists, active since Neo Discovery (2000). Morii hand-sculpts Pokemon figures in polymer clay, builds miniature sets with real pebbles and leaves, and photographs them. With over 209 cards illustrated across more than two decades, her clay-photography method produces what Kotaku called 'warm, tactile' images of 'little creatures living in our world.' [CGC Cards artist showcase: Yuka Morii; Kotaku; hobbylark.com: Who Is Yuka Morii]

A separate Nymble Illustration Rare (no. 096/094, illustrated by Nakamura Ippan) exists in the same Phantasmal Flames set — an out-of-number secret rare holo depicting Nymble nestled among flowers. Wargamer noted it as 'the least valuable Illustration Rare from the Phantasmal Flames set' while praising its ambiguous, insectoid quality: 'an unreadable, mysterious expression that leaves viewers uncertain about the creature's emotional state.' [wargamer.com: Nymble Illustration Rare Nakamura Ippan]
